sections.markdown:
# Parameters: &optional, &rest, &key

A lambda list (parameter list) can be much richer than a flat list of names.

* `&optional` — parameters that may be omitted. Each may have a default form: `(name default)`. Optionals after this marker stay positional.
* `&rest` — collects all remaining positional arguments into a list.
* `&key` — keyword arguments. Callers pass `:name value` pairs in any order. Defaults work like `&optional`.
* `&allow-other-keys` — accept (and ignore) unknown keywords.

A parameter spec like `(name default supplied-p)` adds a third variable that is `T` if the caller actually passed a value (useful to distinguish "defaulted" from "explicitly nil").

sections.explain: Pass it positionally to override the default.
&REST collects everything into a list; APPLY then spreads it.
Keyword args are passed as :name value pairs in any order.
Order doesn't matter for keyword arguments.

sections.prompt: Define POW that takes a base and an &optional exponent (default 2). Return base raised to exponent.
Define MAX-OF that takes any number of integers via &rest and returns the largest. Assume at least one argument.
Define MAKE-URL that takes a :host and an :optional :port (default 80) and returns the string "host:port".
